default.consequence: Every attempt to protect themselves creates the loneliness they fear.
default.inciting_relationship: Someone enters their life who refuses to leave.
default.central_conflict: They must choose between safety and connection.
default.narrative_consequence: They push away the people most capable of helping them.
default.conflict_created: Their defense mechanism actively harms their closest relationships.
default.pressure_point: A crisis forces them to choose between their protection and the person they love.
default.transformation_path: They must learn to drop their facade and accept vulnerability.
default.dramatic_potential: Because this character fears their wound, they protect themselves. The more someone cares about them, the more likely they are to push them away. This creates the central emotional conflict of the story.

independence.consequence: Every attempt to protect themselves creates the loneliness they fear.
independence.relationship_pattern: Pushes away those who try to help.
independence.inciting_relationship: Someone enters their life who refuses to leave.
independence.central_conflict: They must choose between safety and connection.
independence.narrative_consequence: They push away the people most capable of helping them.
independence.conflict_created: Their defense mechanism actively harms their closest relationships.
independence.pressure_point: A crisis forces them to choose between their protection and the person they love.
independence.transformation_path: They must learn to drop their facade and accept vulnerability.
independence.dramatic_potential: Because this character fears abandonment, they protect themselves through independence. The more someone cares about them, the more likely they are to push them away. This creates the central emotional conflict of the story.

perfection.consequence: Every attempt to be flawless creates pressure they cannot survive.
perfection.relationship_pattern: Hides their true self to maintain an image.
perfection.inciting_relationship: Someone sees through their perfect facade and accepts their flaws.
perfection.central_conflict: They must choose between perfection and authenticity.
perfection.narrative_consequence: They push away the people most capable of helping them.
perfection.conflict_created: Their defense mechanism actively harms their closest relationships.
perfection.pressure_point: A crisis forces them to choose between their protection and the person they love.
perfection.transformation_path: They must learn to drop their facade and accept vulnerability.
perfection.dramatic_potential: Because this character fears judgment, they protect themselves through perfectionism. The closer someone gets, the harder they work to maintain the facade. This creates the central emotional conflict of the story.

control.consequence: Every attempt to stay in control pushes them further from real connection.
control.relationship_pattern: Dictates terms of engagement to avoid vulnerability.
control.inciting_relationship: An unpredictable element forces them to surrender control.
control.central_conflict: They must choose between control and trust.
control.narrative_consequence: They push away the people most capable of helping them.
control.conflict_created: Their defense mechanism actively harms their closest relationships.
control.pressure_point: A crisis forces them to choose between their protection and the person they love.
control.transformation_path: They must learn to drop their facade and accept vulnerability.
control.dramatic_potential: Because this character fears chaos, they protect themselves through control. The more they try to control their environment, the more chaotic their relationships become. This creates the central emotional conflict of the story.

summary.story_engine: This is not a character trait. This is a story waiting to happen.
summary.story_beginning: Because this character fears their deepest wound, they protect themselves through their lie.

false_self.narrative_consequence: They keep the mask intact even when honesty would create the connection they need.
false_self.conflict_created: The story conflict emerges when the false self that protects them begins costing them the chance to be truly known.
false_self.pressure_point: A relationship or crisis forces them to choose between maintaining the performance and revealing what is underneath.
false_self.transformation_path: They must risk being seen without the role, charm, or performance that has kept them safe.
false_self.consequence: The more convincing the mask becomes, the less anyone can reach the person behind it.
false_self.relationship_pattern: Performs closeness while hiding the truth.
false_self.inciting_relationship: Someone begins responding to the real self behind the mask.
false_self.central_conflict: They must choose between being accepted as a performance and being known as themselves.
false_self.dramatic_potential: Because this character survives by masking their true self, every intimate moment becomes a test of performance. The more someone gets close, the harder it becomes to keep the false self intact. This creates a story about whether being loved is worth the risk of being seen.

default.consequence: The more they try to protect each other, the less they truly know each other.
default.risk: If neither speaks honestly, they may lose the relationship they are both trying to save.

hiding.consequence: The more they try to protect each other, the less they truly know each other.
hiding.risk: If neither speaks honestly, they may lose the relationship they are both trying to save.

distance.consequence: Their attempts to give each other space has created an unbridgeable gulf.
distance.risk: They may drift so far apart that the connection is completely severed.
distance.pattern: Intimacy is replaced by polite distance.

fixing.consequence: Their need to fix each other prevents them from accepting each other.
fixing.risk: They may destroy the relationship by trying to perfect it.
fixing.pattern: Support has become a form of control.

protective_independence.name: Protective Independence
protective_independence.insight: A possible pattern is that they refuse help before anyone can decide they are too much to carry.
protective_independence.supporting_text: Your answers point toward distance, self-reliance, and the habit of handling danger alone.
protective_independence.next_discovery_hint: Possible cost: the more they protect their independence, the harder it becomes for anyone to reach them.

conditional_worth.name: Conditional Worth
conditional_worth.insight: A possible pattern is that they perform, prove, and perfect themselves when they are afraid love will be withdrawn.
conditional_worth.supporting_text: Your answers point toward worth being earned through achievement, usefulness, or flawlessness.
conditional_worth.next_discovery_hint: Possible cost: they may become admired for the mask while feeling unseen underneath it.

protective_control.name: Protective Control
protective_control.insight: A possible pattern is that they control the room before the room can surprise or expose them.
protective_control.supporting_text: Your answers point toward planning, certainty, and a fear of what happens when someone else has power.
protective_control.next_discovery_hint: Possible cost: the tighter they hold the story, the less room remains for trust.

protective_invisibility.name: Protective Invisibility
protective_invisibility.insight: A possible pattern is that they hide in plain sight, staying useful or quiet so no one looks too closely.
protective_invisibility.supporting_text: Your answers point toward avoidance of attention, emotional cover, and the safety of not being noticed.
protective_invisibility.next_discovery_hint: Possible cost: they may avoid danger by also avoiding being truly known.

fear_abandonment.name: Fear of Abandonment
fear_abandonment.insight: A possible pattern is that they leave first, test loyalty, or pull away because waiting to be left feels worse.
fear_abandonment.supporting_text: Your answers point toward separation, uncertainty, and connection that feels temporary even when it is offered.
fear_abandonment.next_discovery_hint: Possible cost: they may create the distance they were trying to survive.

fear_being_known.name: Fear of Being Known
fear_being_known.insight: A possible pattern is that they change the subject, withhold the truth, or stay charming when closeness asks for honesty.
fear_being_known.supporting_text: Your answers point toward wanting connection while resisting the exposure connection requires.
fear_being_known.next_discovery_hint: Possible cost: people may love the version they show while never meeting the part that needs love most.

emotional_defense.name: Emerging Behavior
emotional_defense.insight: Your answers seem to point toward a repeated protective behavior, not just a private wound.
emotional_defense.supporting_text: Look for what this character does when they feel misunderstood, unwanted, exposed, or unsafe.
emotional_defense.next_discovery_hint: Possible cost: the behavior that helps them survive may also block the relationship or choice their story needs.

abandonment_as_certainty.name: Abandonment as Certainty
abandonment_as_certainty.insight: A possible pattern is that they expect people to leave, so they stop trusting permanence before it can disappoint them.
abandonment_as_certainty.supporting_text: Your answers point toward being left, bracing for loss, and treating attachment as temporary.
abandonment_as_certainty.next_discovery_hint: Possible cost: they may reject real devotion because it arrives too late to feel believable.

clinging_as_survival.name: Clinging as Survival
clinging_as_survival.insight: A possible pattern is that they hold on tighter when connection feels uncertain.
clinging_as_survival.supporting_text: Your answers point toward searching, pursuing, checking, and needing proof that someone will stay.
clinging_as_survival.next_discovery_hint: Possible cost: the more they pursue reassurance, the more fragile the relationship can become.

fear_of_abandonment.name: Fear of Abandonment
fear_of_abandonment.insight: A possible pattern is that they read distance as a warning and react before the loss actually happens.
fear_of_abandonment.supporting_text: Your answers point toward waiting for departure, testing closeness, or protecting against being left.
fear_of_abandonment.next_discovery_hint: Possible cost: they may turn ordinary uncertainty into the very separation they fear.

preemptive_withdrawal.name: Preemptive Withdrawal
preemptive_withdrawal.insight: A possible pattern is that they withdraw first so no one else gets the final word.
preemptive_withdrawal.supporting_text: Your answers point toward silence, distance, and choosing absence before rejection can arrive.
preemptive_withdrawal.next_discovery_hint: Possible cost: they may call it safety while the story reads it as loneliness.

testing_loyalty.name: Testing Loyalty
testing_loyalty.insight: A possible pattern is that they test people instead of asking directly whether they are loved.
testing_loyalty.supporting_text: Your answers point toward watching, waiting, provoking, or setting hidden trials for connection.
testing_loyalty.next_discovery_hint: Possible cost: the people who care may begin to feel judged instead of trusted.

anxious_attachment.name: Anxious Attachment
anxious_attachment.insight: A possible pattern is that they search for proof of closeness whenever silence leaves room for doubt.
anxious_attachment.supporting_text: Your answers point toward pursuing, checking, and reading small distances as emotional danger.
anxious_attachment.next_discovery_hint: Possible cost: reassurance may never last long enough to let them rest.

attachment_through_crisis.name: Attachment Through Crisis
attachment_through_crisis.insight: A possible pattern is that they feel closest to others when something is going wrong.
attachment_through_crisis.supporting_text: Your answers point toward rescue, urgency, and connection that becomes clearest under pressure.
attachment_through_crisis.next_discovery_hint: Possible cost: peace may feel less intimate than crisis.

avoidant_attachment.name: Avoidant Attachment
avoidant_attachment.insight: A possible pattern is that they step back when closeness starts asking for vulnerability.
avoidant_attachment.supporting_text: Your answers point toward independence, silence, and distance as a way to stay in control.
avoidant_attachment.next_discovery_hint: Possible cost: people may mistake their fear for indifference.

conditional_trust.name: Conditional Trust
conditional_trust.insight: A possible pattern is that they trust only while the other person keeps passing invisible tests.
conditional_trust.supporting_text: Your answers point toward guarded permission, watchfulness, and affection that must keep proving itself.
conditional_trust.next_discovery_hint: Possible cost: trust may become a contract no one knows how to fulfill.

trust_destroyed.name: Trust Destroyed
trust_destroyed.insight: A possible pattern is that they treat new tenderness as if it already contains the old betrayal.
trust_destroyed.supporting_text: Your answers point toward broken faith, suspicion, and closeness filtered through past harm.
trust_destroyed.next_discovery_hint: Possible cost: they may punish the present for what the past did.

conditional_worth.name: Conditional Worth
conditional_worth.insight: A possible pattern is that they perform value before anyone can decide they are not enough.
conditional_worth.supporting_text: Your answers point toward earning love, proving usefulness, and hiding need behind achievement.
conditional_worth.next_discovery_hint: Possible cost: they may become needed without ever feeling chosen.

invisible_unless_useful.name: Invisible Unless Useful
invisible_unless_useful.insight: A possible pattern is that they become useful whenever they fear they are otherwise easy to overlook.
invisible_unless_useful.supporting_text: Your answers point toward service, competence, and the belief that presence must be justified.
invisible_unless_useful.next_discovery_hint: Possible cost: they may confuse being relied on with being loved.

love_through_performance.name: Love Through Performance
love_through_performance.insight: A possible pattern is that they keep performing the self others praise, even when it costs them honesty.
love_through_performance.supporting_text: Your answers point toward applause, achievement, and a fear that affection depends on output.
love_through_performance.next_discovery_hint: Possible cost: success may become another way to stay unknown.

worth_through_usefulness.name: Worth Through Usefulness
worth_through_usefulness.insight: A possible pattern is that they offer solutions before anyone can notice their need.
worth_through_usefulness.supporting_text: Your answers point toward helping, fixing, and becoming indispensable as emotional cover.
worth_through_usefulness.next_discovery_hint: Possible cost: the more useful they become, the less permission they feel to be vulnerable.

duty_over_desire.name: Duty Over Desire
duty_over_desire.insight: A possible pattern is that they choose duty before desire has a chance to speak.
duty_over_desire.supporting_text: Your answers point toward obligation, restraint, and refusing what they want because wanting feels dangerous.
duty_over_desire.next_discovery_hint: Possible cost: the story may force them to admit what obedience has been burying.

martyrdom.name: Martyrdom
martyrdom.insight: A possible pattern is that they suffer visibly or silently because sacrifice has become their proof of love.
martyrdom.supporting_text: Your answers point toward endurance, self-denial, and measuring devotion by how much it costs.
martyrdom.next_discovery_hint: Possible cost: they may resent the very people they are trying to protect.

rescuer_identity.name: Rescuer Identity
rescuer_identity.insight: A possible pattern is that they rescue others before anyone can ask what they need.
rescuer_identity.supporting_text: Your answers point toward saving, fixing, and becoming necessary in moments of pain.
rescuer_identity.next_discovery_hint: Possible cost: they may be valued for rescue while remaining emotionally unseen.

sacrifice_as_proof_of_love.name: Sacrifice as Proof of Love
sacrifice_as_proof_of_love.insight: A possible pattern is that they give up pieces of themselves to prove the relationship matters.
sacrifice_as_proof_of_love.supporting_text: Your answers point toward surrender, loyalty, and love measured through loss.
sacrifice_as_proof_of_love.next_discovery_hint: Possible cost: love may start requiring disappearance.

self_worth_through_suffering.name: Self-Worth Through Suffering
self_worth_through_suffering.insight: A possible pattern is that they endure pain because ease feels undeserved.
self_worth_through_suffering.supporting_text: Your answers point toward guilt, punishment, and mistaking suffering for moral proof.
self_worth_through_suffering.next_discovery_hint: Possible cost: they may resist happiness even when the story offers it.

emotional_numbness.name: Emotional Numbness
emotional_numbness.insight: A possible pattern is that they go quiet inside when feeling too much would demand a response.
emotional_numbness.supporting_text: Your answers point toward shutting down, staying composed, and surviving by feeling less.
emotional_numbness.next_discovery_hint: Possible cost: numbness may protect them from pain while also muting love.

openness_as_danger.name: Openness as Danger
openness_as_danger.insight: A possible pattern is that they treat honesty like an unlocked door someone could use against them.
openness_as_danger.supporting_text: Your answers point toward secrecy, caution, and fear around being emotionally exposed.
openness_as_danger.next_discovery_hint: Possible cost: no one can protect the truth they are never allowed to see.

strength_as_armor.name: Strength as Armor
strength_as_armor.insight: A possible pattern is that they stay strong so no one can see where they are breakable.
strength_as_armor.supporting_text: Your answers point toward endurance, refusal of help, and pride used as cover.
strength_as_armor.next_discovery_hint: Possible cost: they may be admired for strength while left alone with pain.

vulnerability_as_chaos.name: Vulnerability as Chaos
vulnerability_as_chaos.insight: A possible pattern is that they avoid emotional openness because one honest feeling might undo the order they rely on.
vulnerability_as_chaos.supporting_text: Your answers point toward control, restraint, and fear of what happens when emotion spills over.
vulnerability_as_chaos.next_discovery_hint: Possible cost: they may keep life orderly by keeping intimacy shallow.

fear_of_burdening.name: Fear of Burdening
fear_of_burdening.insight: A possible pattern is that they hide their needs so love never has to carry them.
fear_of_burdening.supporting_text: Your answers point toward silence, apology, and shrinking their pain before anyone can call it too much.
fear_of_burdening.next_discovery_hint: Possible cost: the people who love them may never know where help is needed.

idealized_love.name: Idealized Love
idealized_love.insight: A possible pattern is that they chase the idea of love when the real person becomes complicated.
idealized_love.supporting_text: Your answers point toward longing, projection, and preferring the imagined bond to the difficult one.
idealized_love.next_discovery_hint: Possible cost: reality may feel like betrayal whenever it stops matching the dream.

love_as_dangerous_power.name: Love as Dangerous Power
love_as_dangerous_power.insight: A possible pattern is that they pull away when love starts having the power to change them.
love_as_dangerous_power.supporting_text: Your answers point toward attraction, fear, and treating intimacy as something that can take control.
love_as_dangerous_power.next_discovery_hint: Possible cost: they may resist the relationship that could transform the story.

love_as_loss_of_self.name: Love as Loss of Self
love_as_loss_of_self.insight: A possible pattern is that they fear closeness will ask them to disappear.
love_as_loss_of_self.supporting_text: Your answers point toward merging, surrender, and defending identity against intimacy.
love_as_loss_of_self.next_discovery_hint: Possible cost: they may choose isolation to stay intact.

love_as_transaction.name: Love as Transaction
love_as_transaction.insight: A possible pattern is that they give, bargain, or perform because love feels safer when it has terms.
love_as_transaction.supporting_text: Your answers point toward exchange, debt, and affection that must be earned or repaid.
love_as_transaction.next_discovery_hint: Possible cost: tenderness may become negotiation instead of trust.

frozen_in_time.name: Frozen in Time
frozen_in_time.insight: A possible pattern is that they keep returning to the moment of loss instead of stepping into what comes after.
frozen_in_time.supporting_text: Your answers point toward memory, refusal of change, and a life organized around what cannot return.
frozen_in_time.next_discovery_hint: Possible cost: the future may feel like betrayal.

grief_as_identity.name: Grief as Identity
grief_as_identity.insight: A possible pattern is that they hold onto grief because letting it soften would change who they believe they are.
grief_as_identity.supporting_text: Your answers point toward loyalty to loss, self-definition through pain, and fear of moving on.
grief_as_identity.next_discovery_hint: Possible cost: healing may feel like abandoning what mattered.

grief_denied.name: Grief Denied
grief_denied.insight: A possible pattern is that they stay busy, useful, or composed so sorrow never gets a clear voice.
grief_denied.supporting_text: Your answers point toward avoidance, performance, and grief displaced into action.
grief_denied.next_discovery_hint: Possible cost: what they refuse to mourn may begin shaping every choice.

hypervigilance.name: Hypervigilance
hypervigilance.insight: A possible pattern is that they scan for danger before anyone else has noticed the room has changed.
hypervigilance.supporting_text: Your answers point toward watching, anticipating, and seeking certainty before trust can form.
hypervigilance.next_discovery_hint: Possible cost: they may mistake calm for the moment before harm.

power_as_safety.name: Power as Safety
power_as_safety.insight: A possible pattern is that they take control before anyone can make them powerless again.
power_as_safety.supporting_text: Your answers point toward dominance, command, and safety built through leverage.
power_as_safety.next_discovery_hint: Possible cost: the people closest to them may experience protection as pressure.

rigidity_as_protection.name: Rigidity as Protection
rigidity_as_protection.insight: A possible pattern is that they cling to rules when uncertainty starts feeling dangerous.
rigidity_as_protection.supporting_text: Your answers point toward structure, refusal, and controlling change before it can hurt them.
rigidity_as_protection.next_discovery_hint: Possible cost: the rule that protects them may become the wall they cannot cross.

belonging_through_caretaking.name: Belonging Through Caretaking
belonging_through_caretaking.insight: A possible pattern is that they care for others before asking whether they are allowed to belong.
belonging_through_caretaking.supporting_text: Your answers point toward helping, watching needs, and earning a place through service.
belonging_through_caretaking.next_discovery_hint: Possible cost: they may be welcomed for what they give, not for who they are.

belonging_through_conformity.name: Belonging Through Conformity
belonging_through_conformity.insight: A possible pattern is that they change shape to fit the room before anyone can reject the real version.
belonging_through_conformity.supporting_text: Your answers point toward masking, agreement, and hiding difference to stay included.
belonging_through_conformity.next_discovery_hint: Possible cost: belonging may require the loss of self.

exile_self_imposed.name: Self-Imposed Exile
exile_self_imposed.insight: A possible pattern is that they stand outside the circle before anyone can push them there.
exile_self_imposed.supporting_text: Your answers point toward distance, outsider identity, and choosing isolation as protection.
exile_self_imposed.next_discovery_hint: Possible cost: exile may become familiar enough to feel like home.

outsider_as_shield.name: Outsider as Shield
outsider_as_shield.insight: A possible pattern is that they turn being different into armor before anyone can use it as a wound.
outsider_as_shield.supporting_text: Your answers point toward defiance, distance, and expecting misunderstanding before it happens.
outsider_as_shield.next_discovery_hint: Possible cost: the shield that protects their difference may also block recognition.

deserved_suffering.name: Deserved Suffering
deserved_suffering.insight: A possible pattern is that they accept pain because part of them believes relief would be unearned.
deserved_suffering.supporting_text: Your answers point toward guilt, punishment, and refusing comfort when it appears.
deserved_suffering.next_discovery_hint: Possible cost: they may mistake suffering for accountability.

endless_apology.name: Endless Apology
endless_apology.insight: A possible pattern is that they apologize before anyone has accused them.
endless_apology.supporting_text: Your answers point toward self-blame, repair attempts, and shrinking to keep peace.
endless_apology.next_discovery_hint: Possible cost: apology may replace honest self-forgiveness.

fear_of_becoming_the_harm.name: Fear of Becoming the Harm
fear_of_becoming_the_harm.insight: A possible pattern is that they hold themselves back because they fear becoming what hurt them.
fear_of_becoming_the_harm.supporting_text: Your answers point toward restraint, avoidance, and suspicion of their own power.
fear_of_becoming_the_harm.next_discovery_hint: Possible cost: they may refuse necessary action because action feels dangerous.

guilt_as_identity.name: Guilt as Identity
guilt_as_identity.insight: A possible pattern is that they keep guilt close because it tells them who they are allowed to be.
guilt_as_identity.supporting_text: Your answers point toward memory, self-judgment, and identity organized around a wrong.
guilt_as_identity.next_discovery_hint: Possible cost: they may resist change because innocence feels impossible.

self_preservation_as_betrayal.name: Self-Preservation as Betrayal
self_preservation_as_betrayal.insight: A possible pattern is that they refuse to save themselves when survival feels like abandoning someone else.
self_preservation_as_betrayal.supporting_text: Your answers point toward loyalty, guilt, and treating personal need as a betrayal.
self_preservation_as_betrayal.next_discovery_hint: Possible cost: love may become a reason not to live fully.

false_self.name: False Self
false_self.insight: A possible pattern is that they present the version that keeps them safe and hide the version that feels too costly to show.
false_self.supporting_text: Your answers point toward performance, concealment, and identity shaped around acceptance.
false_self.next_discovery_hint: Possible cost: the safer self may become a prison.

identity_defined_by_role.name: Identity Defined by Role
identity_defined_by_role.insight: A possible pattern is that they become the role so completely that wanting something else feels like betrayal.
identity_defined_by_role.supporting_text: Your answers point toward duty, usefulness, and selfhood narrowed into a function.
identity_defined_by_role.next_discovery_hint: Possible cost: losing the role may feel like losing the self.

identity_through_opposition.name: Identity Through Opposition
identity_through_opposition.insight: A possible pattern is that they define themselves by resisting what hurt or controlled them.
identity_through_opposition.supporting_text: Your answers point toward defiance, refusal, and a self built against something.
identity_through_opposition.next_discovery_hint: Possible cost: opposition may keep the old wound in charge.

lost_self.name: Lost Self
lost_self.insight: A possible pattern is that they search for direction because their own wanting has gone quiet.
lost_self.supporting_text: Your answers point toward uncertainty, dependence, and difficulty naming what belongs to them.
lost_self.next_discovery_hint: Possible cost: other people may keep writing their story for them.

internalized_unworthiness.name: Internalized Unworthiness
internalized_unworthiness.insight: A possible pattern is that they reject care before anyone can confirm the shame they already believe.
internalized_unworthiness.supporting_text: Your answers point toward hiding, self-denial, and expecting rejection from the inside out.
internalized_unworthiness.next_discovery_hint: Possible cost: love may feel suspicious simply because it contradicts their self-image.

shame_as_secret.name: Shame as Secret
shame_as_secret.insight: A possible pattern is that they protect the secret so carefully that the secrecy begins making their choices.
shame_as_secret.supporting_text: Your answers point toward concealment, silence, and fear of being known through one hidden truth.
shame_as_secret.next_discovery_hint: Possible cost: the secret may gain more power than the truth itself.

shame_driven_achievement.name: Shame-Driven Achievement
shame_driven_achievement.insight: A possible pattern is that they keep achieving so no one has time to notice where they feel unworthy.
shame_driven_achievement.supporting_text: Your answers point toward performance, pressure, and success used as cover.
shame_driven_achievement.next_discovery_hint: Possible cost: achievement may postpone shame without healing it.

shame_masked_as_pride.name: Shame Masked as Pride
shame_masked_as_pride.insight: A possible pattern is that they act untouchable where they actually feel most exposed.
shame_masked_as_pride.supporting_text: Your answers point toward pride, deflection, and confidence used to protect a hidden bruise.
shame_masked_as_pride.next_discovery_hint: Possible cost: people may challenge the pride without ever seeing the pain beneath it.
